Sport|Olympics

Simone Bileshas already secured two gold medals at theParis Olympics, and there is every chance that more follow before the end of the Games.

The 27-year-old was at the heart of USA’s team gold earlier this week, before proving too good in the all-around final to reclaim the title she won eight years ago.

Biles now nine Olympic medals to her name, six of them gold, and the gymnast as looked back to her best in Paris, marking a hugely impressive comeback from her struggles in Tokyo.

With three more individual finals to come, Biles has the chance to make these the most successfulOlympicsof her career, bettering the four golds and one bronze she won in Rio.

Simone Biles will compete in the women’s vault final on Saturday, August 3. That final is scheduled to begin at 3:20pm BST, as Biles goes up against the likes of Rebeca Andrade from Brazil and USA team-mate Jade Carey.

After a day off, Biles will then return for two finals on Monday, August 5.

The American will first compete in the women’s balance beam final, starting at 11:38am, before swiftly turning her focus to the women’s floor exercise final at 1:23pm.

TV channel:The gymnastics finals will be broadcast live in the UK free-to-air on BBC One and on Eurosport.

Live stream:UK viewers can watch online via BBC iPlayer, the BBC Sport website and Discovery+.

Live blog:You can follow all the action from Simone Biles’ remaining events withStandard Sport’s LIVEParis 2024blog.

title: When is Simone Biles competing at Olympics 2024? Start time and TV channel for gymnastics finals | The Standard
author: Matt Verri
description: Biles has three individual finals still to come at Paris 2024
